source_jobs.title: Director of Quality Systems
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html

Job Summary:

We are seeking an experienced Quality Director to lead our manufacturing quality assurance efforts. The ideal candidate has prior experience establishing quality systems from the ground up and is familiar with metalworking processes such as forging, casting, machining, or painting. Experience with multi-stage processes operating on 24/7 schedules and previous work in multi-plant or distributed manufacturing systems is a plus.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Manage the Quality team

 - Establish a company-wide culture of quality through data-driven decision making.

 - Build a Quality Management System (QMS) for defense-grade steel forgings, ensuring ISO 9001 compliance.

 - Lead audits and enforce standards to maintain top-quality, battle-ready products.

 - Use RCCA methodologies (8D, 5-Why) to eliminate defects and non-conformances.

 - Apply Statistical Process Control (SPC) and statistical tools (Minitab, Excel) to maintain process consistency.

 - Utilize metallurgy expertise to verify that steel meets defense specifications.

 - Drive lean and Six Sigma initiatives to reduce waste and improve quality.

 - Serve as the main quality point of contact for customers and suppliers.

 - Oversee four shift supervisors and multiple quality technicians across four shifts.



Required Qualifications:

 - Education: Bachelor’s degree in Quality Systems, Reliability Engineering, Applied Statistics, Industrial Engineering, Metallurgy, or Materials Science.

 - Experience: 7-10+ years in quality management; experience building ISO 9001/AS9100 systems; background in defense, ammunition, aerospace, or complex manufacturing/assembly environments is a plus.

 - Technical Skills: Proficiency with RCCA tools; SPC statistics (Minitab); experience with R-Shiny.

 - Leadership/Work Style: Hands-on leader, team builder, and thrives under pressure.

 - Soft Skills: Strong communicator, proactive problem solver, coach, and mentor.

 - Physical Requirements: Ability to stand, walk, and lift up to 50 lbs in a demanding shop environment with heat and noise.



Preferred Qualifications:

 - Knowledge of defense standards (MIL-STD, ITAR).

 - Mastery of metallurgy for ferrous, non-ferrous, and super alloys.

 - Experience with Nondestructive Testing (NDT), including ultrasonic and dye penetrant methods.

 - Six Sigma Black Belt or ASQ CQE certification.



Work Environment:

This role is primarily based on the shop floor in Dallas, with occasional office work and travel required for audits or customer operations.

source_jobs.title: Senior Manufacturing Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html



Job Description: Senior Manufacturing Engineer

As a Senior Manufacturing Engineer your primary responsibility will be to take ownership of one or more manufacturing cells and manage that cells manufacturing process from beginning to end. Taking a hands-on approach, a successful Senior Manufacturing Engineer will monitor production and testing, assist in troubleshooting manufacturing issues, and drive continuous improvement efforts throughout the process.

Responsibilities:

 - Develop manufacturing methods and process drawings using standard GD&T practices and technical work instructions

 - MUST have metal working experience

 - Maintain revision control and revision changes for manufacturing process drawings

 - Review and approve manufacturing drawings for correct GD&T and accuracy

 - Ability to read and understand technical engineering drawings, specifications, and customer flow down requirements

 - Support the research, design, and selection of tooling, fixturing, and gauging to be used in the manufacturing process

 - Investigate and determine root cause using 8D and 5 why methods

 - Provide technical guidance in the disposition of non-conformances

 - Interpret inspection data during development phases and provide guidance

 - Support commercial quote activity by assessing technical feasibility and providing engineering calculations

 - Provide additional support to the engineering department as needed

 - Other duties as assigned

Skills and Preferred Knowledge

 - Knowledge of metals

 - Knowledge of Non-Destructive Testing including Magnetic Particle, Pressure Testing, and Dye Penetrant

 - Knowledge of Mechanical Testing including Tensile Test, Hardness Test, Ultrasonic Test, Magnetic Particle Test

 - Having a hands-on approach with assembly of designs and fixturing

 - Knowledge of Lean Manufacturing principles

 - Ability to use basic inspection tools such as micrometers, caliper, and dial indicators

 - Excellent interpersonal, written, organizational and verbal communication skills

 - Ability to multitask with a focus on time management to meet schedules

 - A high attention to detail with a mechanical aptitude

 - Proficiency using Microsoft Office (Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Visio and Project)

Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering or equivalent field

 - Minimum 8 years of engineering experience, preferably within a manufacturing environment

 - Experience with ISO9001/AS9100

 - Strong experience in process drawings and GD&T per ASME Y14.5 standards

Physical Demands:

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by and employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to:

 - Walk; sit; stand; bend; lift; reach with hands and arms; stoop; kneel; crouch; crawl; use hands to fingers; handle or feel objects; and move continually during working hours

 - Lift over 50 pounds, along with over the head lifting

 - Vision abilities, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust and focus

 - Able to work flexible hours – including over-time, and weekends to meet customer demands

Personal Protective Equipment - typically required to perform the essent

source_jobs.title: Senior Automation Controls Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html


JOB SUMMARY:

We are seeking a Senior / Lead Automation Engineer to architect, implement, and continuously refine full‑scale automation solutions across our advanced manufacturing operations. You will own the automation roadmap—from concept and capital justification through deployment, ramp, and lifecycle optimization—driving safety, productivity, and quality at enterprise scale.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Automation Strategy & Roadmap

 - Define multi‑year automation vision aligned with business objectives and CapEx plans.

 - Prioritize high‑impact projects using ROI, risk, and throughput models.

 - Present strategic proposals to executive leadership and investors.

System Design & Integration

 - Lead end‑to‑end design of robotic cells (e.g., Fanuc, ABB, KUKA) and custom machines for welding, machining, material handling, inspection, and packaging.

 - Specify PLCs, HMIs, motion control, and safety systems; author URS and functional specs.

 - Oversee vendor selection, FAT/SAT, and site acceptance.

Operational Excellence & Continuous Improvement

 - Drive OEE, cycle‑time, and yield improvements through data analytics and iterative tuning.

 - Implement predictive maintenance, IIoT, and machine‑learning initiatives.

Compliance & Leadership

 - Mentor a team of controls engineers, technicians, and external integrators.

 - Cultivate a culture of disciplined experimentation—“measured power, intelligently applied.”

 - Lead by example, - go to the factory floor and show team members how to solve difficult problems by creating solid architecture and writing good code.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - B.S. in Electrical, Mechanical, Mechatronics, or Controls Engineering (M.S. preferred).

 - 5+ years engineering experience in a project development or manufacturing role.

 - Expert in at least one major robot platform (Fanuc, ABB, KUKA, Yaskawa) and one PLC family (Allen‑Bradley, Siemens).

 - Strong grasp of OT networking (EtherNet/IP, Profinet, OPC UA) and MES/SCADA integration.

 - Proven leadership of cross‑functional teams and external integrators.

 - Excellent communication skills—able to translate technical detail into business impact for executives and investors.




PREFERRED / BONUS SKILLS

 - 10+ years progressive experience in automation, robotics, or advanced manufacturing; 5+ years in a lead or staff‑level role.

 - Experience in defense or aerospace manufacturing environments (AS9100, ITAR).

 - Familiarity with metal forging manufacturing, machine vision, or AI‑driven process control.

 - PMP or equivalent project‑management certification.

source_jobs.title: Metallurgist
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html


POSITION SUMMARY

The Metallurgist is responsible for providing technical expertise in materials science and metallurgical processes to support UNION’s manufacturing operations, quality assurance, and product development. As UNION scales its Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model for defense and industrial production, this role plays a critical part in ensuring material integrity, qualifying new processes, and resolving metallurgical issues across a facility built for continuous readiness and rapid retooling.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Define alloy chemistries, heat-treat cycles, and processing parameters to meet strength, toughness, and fatigue requirements for defense and high-reliability applications.

 - Evaluate and characterize raw materials, forgings, castings, and finished components using metallurgical analysis techniques including microscopy, hardness testing, and tensile/impact evaluation.

 - Lead first-article inspections and metallurgical evaluations to ensure compliance with military, aerospace, and customer-specific standards.

 - Implement statistical process control (SPC) and statistical analyses (CpK/PpK) using tools such as Minitab or JMP to monitor and drive improvement of metallurgical processes.

 - Establish and maintain welding and joining procedures (WPS/PQR) for ferrous and non-ferrous alloys.

 - Conduct material failure investigations using standard laboratory and analytical techniques; drive corrective actions through 5-Why and other structured problem-solving methods.

 - Serve as the primary metallurgical resource for design, production, and supplier quality teams; support supplier audits, source inspections, and customer technical reviews.

 - Review and approve material certifications, test reports, and supplier qualifications.

 - Support new product development and process qualification activities from a materials perspective.

 - Stay current with applicable industry and regulatory standards (ASTM, AMS, ASME, MIL-STD, ITAR) and ensure they are accurately reflected in manufacturing processes and documentation.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Metallurgical Engineering, Materials Science, or a closely related field

 - Minimum 5 years of applied metallurgy experience in defense, aerospace, or high-reliability manufacturing.

 - Expertise in heat-treat design and control for steels and superalloys.

 - Proficiency in metallographic preparation and analysis; mechanical testing (tensile, impact, hardness).

 - Working knowledge of welding metallurgy and residual-stress considerations.

 - Experience with statistical process control and data analysis tools (Minitab, JMP, or equivalent).

 - Strong cross-functional communicator with the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Familiarity with MIL-STD, AMS, and ITAR requirements.

 - Experience with non-destructive testing methods (UT, PT, RT).

 - Six Sigma Green Belt or Black Belt, ASQ CQE, or equivalent certification.

 - Exposure to additive manufacturing metallurgy.

 - Experience in forging, casting, or precision machining environments.

 - Experience with PFMEA and quality management systems (ISO 9001 / AS9100/NADCAP).




WORKING CONDITIONS

Work is performed in both laboratory and manufactu

source_jobs.title: Manufacturing Engineer - CNC Operations
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html

Job Summary

We’re looking for a battle-ready Manufacturing Engineer (CNC Operations) who thrives in the chaos of high-volume precision machining. You’ll own CNC process development, path planning, and tool optimization across a fleet of multi-axis machines—engineering components that defend the U.S. homeland with precision measured in microns. If you can plan complex toolpaths, tune feeds and speeds by instinct, and make machines sing under pressure, we want you in the fight.



Key Responsibilities

• Lead CNC path planning and G-code generation for high-precision parts (lathe work required; 5-axis a plus).

• Program and optimize CNC operations in Mastercam—from toolpath strategy to post-processing.

• Select and qualify cutting tools, inserts, and materials for maximum performance and efficiency.

• Design and validate custom workholding and fixturing to reduce setup time and increase throughput.

• Analyze cycle times, tool wear, and process variation across multiple machines to drive improvements.

• Integrate and optimize robotic part loading/unloading systems and automated machining cells.

• Troubleshoot CNC, tooling, and programming issues in real time with production teams.

• Create and maintain setup sheets, standard work instructions (SWIs), and process documentation under ISO 9001 standards.

• Partner with maintenance and engineering teams to support factory acceptance testing (FAT) and continuous improvement initiatives.



Required Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s in Manufacturing, Mechanical, or Industrial Engineering (or equivalent experience).

Experience: 5+ years in CNC machining environments with proven high-volume production optimization.

 - Deep experience in lathe operations; exposure to 5-axis machining highly desirable.

 - Demonstrated ability to manage multiple machines and custom workholding setups.

Technical Skills:

 - Mastercam (mandatory).

 - G-code creation and modification (Fanuc, Siemens, or Okuma controls).

 - Tool selection and material optimization for steels, alloys, and high-temp materials.

 - Process troubleshooting and cycle-time reduction.

 - CAD/CAM integration using SolidWorks or AutoCAD.

Work Style: Hands-on, detail-obsessed, and relentless in pursuit of efficiency.

Soft Skills: Analytical thinker; collaborative under pressure; strong communicator on the shop floor.



Preferred Qualifications

• Okuma control experience (a plus).

• Exposure to integrated robotics and automated machining cells.

• Familiarity with ERP/MRP and DNC systems.

• Background in defense, aerospace, or high-precision manufacturing (preferred but not required).



Work Environment

You’ll be embedded in our CNC operations center—surrounded by high-speed machining, robotics integration, and a team obsessed with operational excellence. Some travel may be required for supplier qualification and equipment commissioning.



Benefits

• Competitive salary

• Paid time off + holidays

• Career growth in a fast-scaling defense manufacturing startup

source_jobs.title: Senior Process Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Develop and qualify robust industrial processes for defense-grade steel forgings, ensuring first article compliance with defense standards.

 - Perform first article inspections (FAI) and process validations to deliver battle-ready products.

 - Use CpK/PpK statistical analysis and tools (8D, 5-Why) to ensure process capability and stability.

 - Apply SPC and stats tools (Minitab, Excel, R-Shiny) to maintain tight process control.

 - Leverage expertise in metal processing, including heat treating and chemical coatings,
   
   to meet defense specifications.

 - Utilize Lean and Six Sigma methodologies to optimize processes and eliminate
   
   inefficiencies.

 - Partner with operations, quality, safety and engineering teams on short term and long term improvement efforts




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS:

 - US Person - Must be a US Citizen or green card holder

 - Education: Bachelor’s in Process Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Metallurgy,
   
   Materials Science, or Chemical Engineering.

 - Experience: 3-5+ years in process engineering; working in processes for defense, aerospace, or complex manufacturing/assembly; experience with first article inspections is a plus.

 - Technical Skills: CpK/PPK analysis, SPC, RCCA tools (8D, 5-Why), JMP and Python.

 - Work Style: Hands-on, on the floor problem solving, team support

 - Soft Skills: Strong communicator; proactive problem-solver, willing to learn and grow

 - Physical Requirements: Stand, walk, lift 50 lbs in a gritty shop with heat and noise.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Defense standards knowledge (MIL-STD, ITAR).

 - Expertise in metal processing, including heat treating and chemical coatings for ferrous,
   
   non-ferrous, and super alloys.

 - Experience with non-destructive testing (NDT) methods (ultrasonic, dye penetrant) for
   
   process validation.

 - Six Sigma Black Belt or ASQ Certified Manufacturing Engineer (CMfgE).




WORK ENVIRONMENT:

You’ll fight on the shop floor in Dallas, with occasional office time and travel for process

validations or customer ops.

source_jobs.title: DevOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html


WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

In defense manufacturing, system reliability and compliance are inseparable. Every deployment must uphold confidentiality, integrity, and availability under CMMC Level 2 and ITAR requirements. As a DevOps Engineer, you will design, automate, and maintain secure, high-availability infrastructure that supports mission-critical applications both on-premises and in the cloud. You’ll report to the Director of IT and collaborate closely with software engineers, QA, and security teams to ensure that every build, deployment, and runtime environment meets the highest standards for security and compliance.



What You’ll Do

 - Design, implement, and manage Kubernetes clusters (on-prem and cloud) for secure, containerized workloads.

 - Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions to enable controlled, automated deployments across environments.

 - Develop infrastructure-as-code solutions using Ansible, Helm, and other automation tools.

 - Secure containers and clusters with hardened baselines (DoD STIG/FIPS) and continuous compliance scanning.

 - Implement logging, monitoring, and alerting across infrastructure using open-source or commercial observability stacks.

 - Automate image builds and vulnerability scanning in accordance with CMMC / ITAR controls.

 - Manage configuration drift, access control, and audit trails in line with NIST 800-171 and CMMC Level 2.

 - Collaborate with software, QA, and security teams to integrate DevSecOps principles throughout the SDLC.

 - Optimize build and deployment workflows for reliability, reproducibility, and speed in regulated environments.

 - Participate in on-call rotations and incident response to maintain uptime and security posture.



The Tools You’ll Work With

 - Containerization & Orchestration: Kubernetes (on-prem and cloud), Docker, Helm, Kafka

 - Automation & Configuration: Ansible, Bash/Python

 - CI/CD & Version Control: GitHub Actions, GitHub Enterprise

 - Monitoring & Logging: Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, ELK / Graylog

 - Security & Compliance: Vault, FIPS-validated cryptography, CIS Benchmarks, STIG checklists

 - OS & Environments: Red Hat / Oracle Linux 9, Ubuntu 22.04, hardened images, Windows Server, Active Directory

 - Networking & Storage: iSCSI/NFS, reverse proxy + TLS/mTLS



Required Qualifications

 - 4 + years of hands-on experience in DevOps, SRE, or infrastructure engineering.

 - Proven expertise managing Kubernetes clusters and containerized applications in production.

 - Strong knowledge of CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions or similar systems.

 - Proficiency in Linux administration, Bash/Python scripting, and infrastructure automation (Ansible, Terraform).

 - Experience securing and hardening Linux servers and container images (STIG, FIPS, CIS).

 - Familiarity with CMMC Level 2, NIST 800-171, or ITAR compliance requirements.

 - Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot complex build and deployment pipelines.

 - Excellent documentation, collaboration, and problem-solving skills.



Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience managing hybrid or air-gapped Kubernetes environments.

 - Background in DoD-aligned infrastructure or regulated manufacturing environments.

 - Experience integrating security scanning tools (Trivy, Anchore, Grype) into CI/CD.

 - Fa

source_jobs.title: Controls Engineering Technician
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html


JOB SUMMARY:

You are Controls Engineering Technician at Union Factory 1, your primary responsibility is to support the installation, maintenance, troubleshooting, and optimization of automation and control systems across manufacturing operations. This role works closely with the Staff Electrical and Control Engineer and cross-functional teams to ensure reliable operation of PLC-based systems, HMIs, and industrial automation equipment. A successful Controls Engineering Technician will demonstrate strong technical aptitude, hands on troubleshooting skills, and a commitment to maintaining safe and efficient operations.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Support installation, wiring, and commissioning of PLC-based control systems, HMIs, and industrial automation equipment

 - Assist in troubleshooting and resolving electrical and controls-related issues on manufacturing equipment

 - Perform routine maintenance and diagnostics on control systems, sensors, drives, and instrumentation

 - Support debugging and minor modifications to PLC programs under the direction of Controls Engineers

 - Read and interpret electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, and panel layouts

 - Assist in building and modifying electrical panels and control systems

 - Support integration of control systems with MES, ERP, and data collection systems as needed

 - Maintain and update controls documentation including wiring diagrams, I/O lists, and system configurations

 - Perform system checks to ensure proper operation of automation equipment

 - Support equipment startups, testing, and commissioning activities

 - Follow all safety procedures including lockout/tagout and electrical safety requirements

 - Collaborate with maintenance, engineering, and production teams to support equipment reliability

 - Assist in identifying and implementing improvements to control systems and processes




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - High school diploma or equivalent.

 - 2 to 5 years of electrical experience in a manufacturing or industrial environment.

 - Demonstrated experience troubleshooting industrial motor circuits and basic controls.

 - Ability to work flexible hours including overtime, weekends, and an on call rotation.

 - Strong understanding of electrical systems and industrial controls

 - Basic knowledge of PLC systems (Allen-Bradley, Siemens, or equivalent)

 - Ability to read and interpret electrical schematics and wiring diagrams

 - Familiarity with industrial network protocols (EtherNet/IP, Modbus, etc.) preferred

 - Experience with sensors, drives, and instrumentation

 - Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills

 - Hands on experience with tools and electrical equipment

 - Good communication and teamwork abilities

 - Ability to work in industrial environments with exposure to machinery and electrical systems

 - Proficiency with Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook)




PREFERRED / BONUS SKILLS

 - Associate/ B.S. degree or technical certificate in electrical, electromechanical, or industrial controls.




PHYSICAL DEMANDS

The physical demands described here are representative of those an employee must meet to perform the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to:

 - Walk, stand, sit, bend, reach,

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineering Technician
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html

As a Mechanical Engineering Technician, you will drive OEE gains on defense-critical production lines. You will partner with Manufacturing, Quality, and Controls to lift reliability, raise first-pass yield, and take time out of cycle. You will support Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT) and Site Acceptance Testing (SAT), including executing protocols, collecting data, and closing punch-list items. Some travel, including international in the first few months, may be required.


What You’ll Do

 - Run and support Kaizen events that improve Availability, First-Pass Yield, and Throughput.

 - Work with engineers to identify and remove bottlenecks on the line and document durable countermeasures.

 - Design, build, and fit simple fixtures and production aids; use shop tools and basic fabrication as needed.

 - Use internal tools to diagnose product and equipment issues, perform root cause analysis, and communicate corrective actions.

 - Draft and update Manufacturing Instructions and assist with training operators and other technicians.

 - Pull and analyze production data to spot patterns and propose solutions using Excel and, where helpful, Python, SQL, Minitab, or JMP.

 - Support FAT/SAT mechanically: instrument stations, run acceptance tests, log results in MES, and help release equipment to production.

 - Perform precision metrology and basic preventive maintenance to protect rate and quality.

What You’ll Bring

 - Hands-on experience with hand and power tools and mechanical assembly, including fasteners, fits, torque methods, and alignment.

 - Working knowledge of pneumatics and hydraulics; basic electro-mechanical skills are a plus (low-voltage sensors, harness repair, DMM use).

 - Ability to read prints, GD&T, and schematics; comfortable interpreting 3D CAD. Experience with SolidWorks or similar is preferred.

 - Exposure to fabrication or prototyping is helpful (machining basics, 3D printing, laser cutting).

 - Proficiency with Microsoft Office and comfort using data tools to support decisions.

 - Clear written and verbal communication; willingness to follow and improve SWIs and safety procedures (LOTO, cranes/rigging, forklifts).

 - Ability to travel, including internationally during early equipment builds and SAT, as needed.

Required Qualifications

 - Experience:
   
   - Tech I: 1–2+ years mechanical assembly/maintenance/prototyping.
   
   - Tech II: 3–5+ years owning setups, alignments, PMs, and quality checks.

 - Tech III (Lead): 6+ years, cross-trade troubleshooting and mentoring.

 - Core skills: Read prints & GD&T; precision assembly to tolerance; shop math; hand/power tools; basic mill/lathe/drill; hydraulics/pneumatics fundamentals; create clear notes/SWIs.

 - Tempo: Demonstrated ability to meet rate and quality targets under time pressure while following SWIs and safety rules.

Preferred

 - CAD literacy for simple fixture edits (SolidWorks or similar); ability to interpret 3D models.

 - Exposure to PLC/VFD basics (status reads, alarm ack), sensors, and I/O checks (controls team will own programming).

 - Experience with heat treat/thermal processes, hardness checks, and distortion control (nice to have for our parts).

 - CMMS/MES and basic SPC; root-cause tools (5-Why, Ishikawa).

 - Certifications: Forklift, overhead crane/riggi

source_jobs.title: Automation Controls Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html

Job Summary

We are seeking an Automation Controls Engineer to design, program, and support automation systems across our manufacturing operations. This role focuses on PLC programming, system integration, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement of automated equipment to ensure safe, efficient, and reliable production.

Key Responsibilities

Controls Design & Programming

 - Develop, program, and troubleshoot PLC, HMI, and motion control systems (Allen-Bradley, Siemens, etc.).

 - Support integration of robotic systems (Fanuc, ABB, KUKA, Yaskawa) and automated equipment.

 - Create and maintain electrical schematics, control narratives, and functional specifications.

System Integration & Support

 - Assist with installation, commissioning, and startup of automated equipment.

 - Work with vendors and integrators during system builds, FAT, and site acceptance.

 - Interface with MES/SCADA systems and plant networks (EtherNet/IP, Profinet, OPC UA).

Troubleshooting & Maintenance

 - Diagnose and resolve controls-related issues on the production floor.

 - Provide ongoing support to minimize downtime and improve system reliability.

 - Implement and maintain safety systems in compliance with standards.

Continuous Improvement

 - Identify opportunities to improve cycle time, efficiency, and equipment performance.

 - Support data collection and basic analysis to improve OEE and reduce waste.

 - Participate in process improvements and equipment upgrades.

Collaboration

 - Work closely with manufacturing, maintenance, and engineering teams.

 - Support technicians and operators in resolving automation-related issues.

 - Contribute to a practical, hands-on engineering culture focused on problem-solving.

Minimum Qualifications

 - B.S. in Electrical, Mechanical, Mechatronics, or Controls Engineering (or equivalent experience).

 - 3–7+ years of experience in industrial automation or controls engineering.

 - Experience with at least one PLC platform (Allen-Bradley or Siemens preferred).

 - Familiarity with industrial robotics and automated systems.

 - Basic understanding of industrial networking and communication protocols.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with HMI/SCADA systems.

 - Exposure to machine vision or advanced automation systems.

 - Experience in manufacturing environments (automotive, aerospace, or similar).

 - Strong troubleshooting and hands-on problem-solving skills.

source_jobs.title: Electrical Engineering Technician
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html


JOB SUMMARY:

You are the hands on extension of site electrical engineering at Union Factory 1. You support the Staff Electrical Engineer in keeping forging, machining, and inspection operations running safely and on schedule. The job exists to reduce unplanned electrical downtime, deliver capital project electrical scope, build and wire in house control panels, and keep site documentation up to date.

Day to day, you walk down equipment, redline as built drawings, execute LOTO, support arc flash data collection, build and wire control panels per Staff EE designs, install and commission capital scope, and troubleshoot faults on CNC machines, forging presses, conveyors, and facility power.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Install, maintain, troubleshoot, and repair electrical systems and components across forging, machining, inspection, and facility equipment.

 - Read and apply electrical schematics, ladder logic, P&IDs, and wiring diagrams. Redline them when reality disagrees.

 - Perform preventive and corrective maintenance on motors, VFDs, control circuits, lighting, distribution gear, and grounding systems.

 - Test electrical systems with multimeters, clamp meters, insulation resistance testers, phase rotation meters, and thermal imagers. Log readings in the CMMS.

 - Support PLC and HMI troubleshooting on Rockwell ControlLogix and CompactLogix and on Siemens TIA Portal platforms. Coordinate code changes with controls engineering.

 - Maintain electrical spares inventory. Flag stockouts and assist with purchasing on parts you specify or use.

 - Comply with the NEC, NFPA 70E, NFPA 79, and site electrical standards. Cite the applicable article when asked.

 - Wear required PPE. Stop work and escalate any unsafe condition.

 - Other duties as assigned by the Staff Electrical Engineer or Maintenance Manager.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - High school diploma or equivalent.

 - 2 to 5 years of electrical experience in a manufacturing or industrial environment.

 - Demonstrated experience troubleshooting industrial motor circuits and basic controls.

 - Ability to work flexible hours including overtime, weekends, and an on call rotation.

 - Industrial electrical systems: 480V three phase distribution, motor controls, VFDs, control transformers, grounding and bonding.

 - Schematic and wiring diagram reading. Comfortable redlining what you find in the field.

 - Electrical troubleshooting in a production environment. Calm under downtime pressure.

 - Control panel building and wiring per UL 508A and NFPA 79 practice: component mounting, wire duct fill, power and signal segregation, grounding and bonding, terminal block selection, wire labeling.

 - Working knowledge of PLC and HMI platforms (Rockwell and/or Siemens). Able to monitor I/O, safely force outputs when authorized, and interpret fault codes.

 - Electrical safety standards: OSHA 1910 Subpart S, NFPA 70E current edition, NEC.

 - Test equipment: DMM, clamp meter, insulation resistance tester (megger), phase rotation meter, power quality analyzer, thermal imager.

 - Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) and CMMS data entry. AutoCAD viewer or full AutoCAD experience for reading and printing drawings.

 - Clear written documentation




PREFERRED / BONUS SKILLS

 - Associate/ B.S. degree or technical

source_jobs.title: Executive Communications Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Union Technologies is reindustrializing America’s defense manufacturing base with a first-of-its-kind Factories-as-a-Stockpile™ model, integrating advanced robotics, manufacturing, and software into sovereign factory systems at scale. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, UNION is dedicated to restoring America’s industrial strength and guaranteeing deterrence for the century ahead.

We recently emerged from stealth with $50M in funding led by top-tier venture investors and strategic partners. Read more in our Press Release https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/union-the-wests-new-arsenal-of-national-security-lethalitythe-worlds-newest-manufacturing-superpower-302443156.html



POSITION SUMMARY

The Executive Communications Specialist owns the materials, narratives, and communications systems that help UNION's Growth team turn complex technical, commercial, and customer context into clear executive communications. This role works directly with the Head of Growth to build customer-ready decks, briefings, one-pagers, leadership updates, and public-facing copy. The right person is a senior individual contributor who brings writing judgment, material-building discipline, and enough commercial context to make high-stakes work easier to understand and act on.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build executive-ready and customer-ready decks, one-pagers, briefings, and follow-up materials for internal, customer, partner, investor, and leadership conversations.

 - Turn technical, manufacturing, software, and commercial inputs into concise storylines, visuals, talking points, and speaker notes.

 - Draft leadership updates, executive emails, fact sheets, company statements, and public-facing copy for review and approval.

 - Maintain reusable Growth materials, approved language blocks, current messaging, and a practical content library.

 - Prepare decision briefs and non-contract materials for customer follow-up, partner coordination, diligence, data rooms, and proposal support.

 - Coordinate with Growth, Capture and Proposals, Government Contracts, Engineering, Manufacturing, Software, Finance, external advisors, and leadership to keep materials accurate, current, and handled with appropriate sensitivity.



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 6+ years in executive communications, strategic communications, product marketing, proposal content, or high-stakes content production.

 - Portfolio of executive-level decks, customer materials, public-facing copy, or strategic narratives.

 - Strong writing, editing, visual storytelling, and judgment with senior audiences.

 - Ability to learn complex technical, industrial, software, and commercial context quickly and make it useful.

 - Proven discipline with version control, approvals, confidentiality, and sensitive information.

 - Ability to work on-site in Dallas, Texas and support occasional travel or time-sensitive executive and customer material needs.



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in defense-adjacent, advanced manufacturing, robotics, industrial technology, or complex enterprise sales environments.

 - Experience supporting proposals, captures, government buyers, prime contractors, investors, or partner diligence.

 - Experience building a reusable content library, sales enablement system, or communications operating model from scratch.



WORKING CONDITIONS

This is an on-site role in Dallas, Texas. The role works closely with Growth, Capture and Proposals, Government Contracts, Engineering, Manufacturing, Software, Finance, external advisors, and executive leadership. Work may involve sensitive commercial, defense-adjacent, partner, customer, and export-controlled information, so strong judgment and disciplined handling are required. Some travel may be required for customer meetings, partner meetings, events, or executive communications support.

notes: Defense manufacturing company with a strong focus on engineering and operations roles.
